summary.intent: Implement GitHub issues #282 (RatingGroup) and #283 (TagGroup) as agnostic-core data-display components in ars-components, with full test coverage, mutation testing, and a reviewed PR.
summary.outcome: Both components were implemented with unit, snapshot, spec-conformance, proptest, and mutation gates passing. A PR (#694) was opened, rebased onto main, and a Codex review was addressed with fixes for prop-sync, pluralized labels, removal announcement payload, disabled deselect guard, and an overly broad mutation exclusion.

repo: Snapshot files must live in component-local `src/.../snapshots/` directories, not under `tests/snapshots/`; insta places them based on where the assertion lives
Unit/contract tests must be colocated with component code in `#[cfg(test)]` blocks inside the module file, not in detached integration test files under `crates/ars-components/tests/`
The repo requires `cargo xci-fast` (10-step gate) before every push
Mutation testing via `cargo xmutants` is a required pre-handoff gate for each new component module; parallel runs are blocked by a shared `mutants.out` lock
`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` is blocked by many pre-existing unreferenced snapshots across other components — cannot be used as a per-PR gate without cleanup of older fixtures
Draft PRs do not trigger CI checks; must be marked ready-for-review to get check results
Codex review is triggered by posting `@codex review` as a PR comment; the 👀 reaction signals it is actively reviewing
RatingGroup spec §2 mentioned `ItemHalf`/`ItemFull` as public `Part` variants but §1.6/§5.2 define the actual public anatomy — the §2 prose describes adapter-rendered visual substructure only
RatingGroup uses `role=radiogroup`/`role=radio` in whole-number mode and `role=slider` with `aria-value*` in half/fractional mode; fractional `step` (not just `allow_half`) triggers slider semantics
TagGroup uses a grid ARIA pattern: `role=grid` on root, `role=row` on list, `role=gridcell` on tag items
Machine `on_props_changed` / prop-sync must be implemented for controlled `Bindable` props to stay coherent when props change after initialization
TagGroup removal effects should carry an explicit payload (removed key label) rather than relying on callers to reconstruct context

code.finding: The fallback percent formatter scaled values but did not append the `%` symbol; main independently fixed this before the branch landed, causing a rebase conflict that was resolved by keeping main's implementation and folding in the fractional-value regression test
Regression test for fallback percent formatting lives here; covers both whole and fractional percent values to prevent recurrence
New RatingGroup state machine; `Bindable<f64>` value, step resolution priority: explicit finite step > allow_half (0.5) > default (1.0), disabled mode blocks all mutations including focus
New TagGroup state machine; `StaticCollection<Tag>` for items, `Bindable<BTreeSet<Key>>` for selection, no-wrap navigation, removal skips group-disabled/item-disabled/missing keys

One TagGroup whole-transition-plan mutant survives only by timeout (replacing the function body makes the entire test suite time out, not fail a specific assertion); added a narrow documented exclusion. Overly broad exclusions were flagged in review and narrowed.

workflow: Tests-first (red → green → mutation → audit) is the required delivery flow for new agnostic-core components
The post-implementation audit skill must be run before handoff and findings must be landed in the same local change
Mutation runs are sequential per workspace due to shared output lock; plan for wall-clock time when two components are under mutation testing
Spec drift found in review: adapter specs (Leptos/Dioxus) must be kept in sync with the core contract changes, including new props like `default_selected_keys`
Moving detached integration tests into module-local `#[cfg(test)]` blocks can surface pre-existing bugs (Meter/Progress percent formatting) that were hidden by test isolation

friction: Mutation testing for two components was extremely slow; multiple polling sessions required, and parallel runs competed for the shared `mutants.out` lock
`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` cannot be used as a clean per-PR snapshot gate because of many pre-existing unreferenced fixtures in other components
The broader `cargo llvm-cov test ... -- data_display` gate failed on pre-existing Meter/Progress snapshot drift unrelated to the new components, requiring a narrower per-integration-test invocation
Draft PR mode silently suppresses CI checks; had to mark ready-for-review to get check results
Codex review took many polling cycles (8+ polls over ~20 minutes) before threads appeared
Rebase conflict in `crates/ars-i18n/src/number/mod.rs` because both branches independently fixed the same percent-formatting bug with slightly different implementations
The `cargo insta test` CLI `--glob-filter` flag did not actually restrict the unreferenced scan to the specified test binary, making the flag misleading
open_items: Leptos and Dioxus adapter implementations for RatingGroup and TagGroup are explicitly out of scope and still unimplemented
Pre-existing unreferenced insta snapshots across other components should be cleaned up so `--unreferenced=reject` can be used as a workspace-wide gate
TagGroup `AnnounceRemoved` effect payload format should be validated against the adapter accessibility announcement API when adapters are implemented
RatingGroup half-step hit-testing geometry and pointer event coordinate math are deferred to adapter layer
Codex review response commit was created but the push was cut off at end of transcript — verify the rebased review-fix branch was force-pushed and Codex re-triggered
